message:
chore(beads): close ft-cke6c — test API drift repaired (65cbb26ba)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Those lanes fail before validating the runtime surface because the file no longer exists. Update executable guard scripts to probe runtime_async.rs while leaving historical docs alone.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T21:49:54.267240Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T21:54:02.530711Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T21:54:02.530222Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 4360d234: E2E runtime guard scripts now target runtime_async.rs and ft-22x4r validation passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-c7q63","title":"[ft-3tvvt] centralize proptest pane-scope runtime block_on","description":"Follow-on from ft-3tvvt. prop_fts_respects_pane_scope creates a multi-thread runtime and block_on per proptest case. Use shared storage proptest async helper.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:16.576250Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:00:16.576250Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-cbeyl","title":"[MEDIUM] mcp_manifest_golden db-gated tool whitelist silently drifts when tool renamed or removed","description":"In commit 1bde42b3 (ft-nnfwn: freeze no-db mcp manifest surface),
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` at
crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_manifest_golden.rs:254-316 hard-codes
three whitelists of tools/resources/templates expected to be db-gated:

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [
    \"wa.accounts\",
    \"wa.events\",
    \"wa.reserve\",
    \"wa.release\",
    \"wa.search\",
    \"wa.send\",
    \"wa.workflow_run\",
] {
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

The assertion only checks absence from \`no_db_tools\`. If a tool is
renamed (e.g., \`wa.accounts\` → \`wa.list_accounts\`) or removed entirely,
the whitelist entry silently stays true (\`!no_db_tools.contains(\"wa.accounts\")\`
is trivially true when \`wa.accounts\` no longer exists in either manifest).

The same drift pattern applies to the resource whitelist
(lines ~285, \`wa://accounts\`, \`wa://events\`, \`wa://reservations\`) and
the template whitelist (lines ~295, \`wa://accounts/{service}\` etc.).

Impact: the no-db subset guard gives false confidence. A tool could
accidentally become always-on (or be removed from db-gated registration)
without the test catching it.

Fix: for each whitelist entry, assert \`full_tools.contains(entry)\`
BEFORE checking \`!no_db_tools.contains(entry)\`. That way, if the
whitelisted tool disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test
fails and forces whoever made the change to update the whitelist
deliberately.

\`\`\`rust
for expected_absent_tool in [...] {
    assert!(
        full_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"whitelist drift: {expected_absent_tool} no longer exists in db-backed manifest — update the test\"
    );
    assert!(
        !no_db_tools.contains(expected_absent_tool),
        \"db-gated tool unexpectedly advertised without db_path: {expected_absent_tool}\"
    );
}
\`\`\`

Severity rationale: medium — test-quality issue that masks regressions
in MCP tool surface categorization. Not a correctness bug in production
code.

Evidence: commit 1bde42b3 diff lines 262-316 of the new
\`mcp_manifest_matches_golden_without_db\` test body.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T07:24:49.960054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915565Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T08:03:23.915363Z","close_reason":"Added full_*.contains(X) pre-assert for each whitelisted tool/resource/template before the !no_db.contains(X) check. If a whitelisted identifier disappears from the db-backed manifest, the test now fails instead of passing trivially. Fix landed in 4d52f550. Verified 6/6 tests pass via rch exec.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","test-gap","testing"]}
-{"id":"ft-cke6c","title":"[test-blocker] cargo test toon blocked by stale Tantivy and TriggerCategoryCounts tests","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core toon is blocked before completing the TOON-filtered suite by pre-existing test API drift outside ft-5490a. First observed failures: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tantivy_integration_tests.rs implements old IndexLookup methods has_event_id/get_log_offset and omits IntegrityCheckConfig.batch_size. A local exploratory repair then exposed a second stale API in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs where chunked_trigger_counts still returns HashMap<TriggerCategory,u64> while ScanPipeline now exposes TriggerCategoryCounts (E0308 at line 370, E0277/PartialEq around lines 471/534/577). The focused TOON golden test passes with rch: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test toon_golden toon.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:09.843723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T19:38:50.572776Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"ft-cke6c","title":"[test-blocker] cargo test toon blocked by stale Tantivy and TriggerCategoryCounts tests","description":"cargo test -p frankenterm-core toon is blocked before completing the TOON-filtered suite by pre-existing test API drift outside ft-5490a. First observed failures: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/tantivy_integration_tests.rs implements old IndexLookup methods has_event_id/get_log_offset and omits IntegrityCheckConfig.batch_size. A local exploratory repair then exposed a second stale API in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/proptest_patterns_metamorphic.rs where chunked_trigger_counts still returns HashMap<TriggerCategory,u64> while ScanPipeline now exposes TriggerCategoryCounts (E0308 at line 370, E0277/PartialEq around lines 471/534/577). The focused TOON golden test passes with rch: cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test toon_golden toon.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:56:09.843723Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T20:06:29.171979Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T20:06:29.171693Z","close_reason":"Six API-drift repairs landed at 65cbb26ba. cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --tests now exits 0 (was blocked by 30+ errors across 6 test files). cargo test --test toon_golden toon → 5 passed. Specific drifts diagnosed + fixed: (1) SearchOptions.include_highlights field added at e2e_capture_search.rs:163; (2) IndexLookup trait combined to single lookup_event_offset across 3 mock impls (recorder_recovery_drills, tantivy_integration_tests, tantivy_reindex_labruntime); (3) IntegrityCheckConfig.batch_size: 1000 added to 13 struct literals across 2 files; (4) CautServiceProviderExt + CautErrorRemediationExt brought into scope in proptest_caut imports for moved provider_hint/from_provider/remediation methods; (5) TriggerCategoryCounts (packed [u64;N] replacing HashMap<TriggerCategory,u64>) wired through proptest_patterns_metamorphic — helper return type, .iter() iteration, PartialEq comparison. Pre-bead state: cod_4 surfaced cargo test toon failing on Tantivy + TriggerCategoryCounts. Post-fix: every test binary the toon filter touches compiles cleanly. Long-running campaign of the full test suite is the deployment step.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-cqhfe","title":"[ft-gkqej/3] Update multi-pass-bug-hunting / mock-code-finder skill templates to emit SHA-anchored frontmatter","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:57:57.639942Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765357Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T03:42:41.765142Z","close_reason":"Appended an 'Audit Marker Output (SHA-anchored frontmatter)' section to both /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/multi-pass-bug-hunting/SKILL.md (between Documentation Template and Convergence Criteria) and /Users/jemanuel/.claude/skills/mock-code-finder/SKILL.md (between Lessons From Real Sessions and Anti-Patterns). Each section: (1) shows the required YAML frontmatter shape (name / description / type / swept_at_sha / sweep_paths / stale_after_commits / findings_summary), (2) explains how to populate each field — captures swept_at_sha via 'git rev-parse HEAD' BEFORE the sweep starts so subsequent fix commits don't shift the anchor, lists resolved sweep_paths not globs, and picks stale_after_commits per churn class (storage/runtime: 30, mux/codec/vendored: 20, patterns/config/DSL: 5), (3) references the audit_marker_doctor.sh helper (frankenterm scripts/, ft-nedq3) and its CI-friendly exit codes 0/1/2, (4) cites the failure mode that motivated the convention — the 2026-04-20 sweep markers describing themselves as 'clean' while their swept paths quietly accumulated 24/91/154 commits. Skill-internal cross-refs explicit. Pure docs work in skill files outside the project repo; no build needed. Skills outside this repo are not synced; the next time skills-revision-sync-to-jeffreys-skills-md runs (or the equivalent), the new sections will propagate.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","process","skills"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-cqhfe","depends_on_id":"ft-gkqej","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T23:58:11.043827Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-cro2u","title":"[LOW] ft-dlyhk policy-gate test skips audit-persist path (868af5ad smell)","description":"Fresh-eyes review of 868af5ad: wait_for_tool_applies_policy_rules_before_waiting passes None for db_path so the persist_mcp_policy_denial_async branch (mcp_tools.rs:1809-1820) is never exercised, despite the commit claiming 'asserts audit record was persisted'. Add a test case that passes a real storage_ref and asserts the audit row appeared.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-24T21:29:17.543808Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.587055Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T01:52:58.586800Z","close_reason":"e5dcdcb4 adds wait_for_tool_persists_policy_denial_audit_when_storage_is_attached, the sibling of the existing storage=None test, that passes Some(Arc<PathBuf>) for db_path and asserts the policy_denied_audit row landed with the correct tool_name / decision / reason_code / reason. While writing the test, surfaced a real storage bug — fresh DBs skip the v24 CREATE TABLE policy_denied_audit — filed as ft-7tq4z [HIGH] with full repro + two-fix sketch + scope notes. Test workarounds the storage drift by pre-seeding the v24 schema via direct rusqlite; when ft-7tq4z lands the workaround can be deleted. Test passes on rch /tmp/ft-cc6-target with --features mcp,asupersync-runtime.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mcp","review","review-of-review","testing"]}
 {"id":"ft-ctt7k","title":"[codebase-audit][capture/storage] WAL checkpoint cost under sustained-write load is unmeasured + ungoverned","description":"## Domain gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` opens SQLite in WAL mode
(per the existing benches and `recorder_storage.rs` — confirmed
by `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL` patterns in the schema setup). WAL
mode trades disk-write durability for read concurrency: writes
go to a sidecar `.db-wal` file; checkpoints fold the WAL back
into the main DB on a periodic schedule (default: when the WAL
hits ~4MB).

## What's missing (not caught by prior audits)

- `benches/recorder_ingest.rs` (proposed in ft-9r82k) covers
  ingest CPU/serialize cost.
- `tests/storage_regression.rs` (34 KB) covers schema regression.
- 9 migration tests + proptest_recorder.* cover migration safety.

**None measure or govern WAL checkpoint cost.** Specifically:

1. **Checkpoint stalls under sustained writes.** SQLite's auto-
   checkpoint runs synchronously on the writer thread when the
   WAL crosses the threshold. At fleet scale (200 panes × 10
   events/sec = 2000 events/sec), the WAL fills in seconds; the
   checkpoint stalls every writer for the duration of the fold.
   p99 latency on the recorder hot path likely has an unbounded
   tail driven by checkpoint cadence.

2. **WAL grows unbounded if checkpoint blocks.** A single
   long-running read query holds the WAL "in use" — auto-
   checkpoint fails, WAL grows past the `journal_size_limit`
   pragma if not configured, and disk fills. ft has no
   explicit configuration of `wal_autocheckpoint`,
   `journal_size_limit`, or `synchronous` PRAGMAs.

3. **No adaptive throttle.** When checkpoints are running long
   (because of blocking readers or just disk pressure), the
   ingest path doesn't know to slow down. Per the README's
   backpressure hierarchy (Green/Yellow/Red/Black), there's
   no checkpoint-cost-aware tier.

This is exactly the SaaS pattern of "fail-open under sustained
load" — but for a local SQLite store rather than Redis.

## Why this catches real defects

Concrete failure scenarios:

- **Operator runs a swarm session for 8 hours.** The WAL
  accumulates because of a long-running `ft search` that
  pinned readers across a checkpoint. Disk fills overnight.
  Recorder writes start failing.
- **`ft session restore` reads from a 100 MB WAL.** Restore
  takes minutes because every read joins the main DB + WAL.
- **Tail latency on the recorder hot path** spikes every 30s
  as auto-checkpoint runs. Per the profiling skill's tail-
  latency discipline, p99 must be measured — today p50 is fine
  but p99 hides a checkpoint stall.

## Suggested approach

1. **Bench**: `benches/wal_checkpoint_cost.rs` —
   - Sustained-write workload (10K events/sec for 60s).
   - Measure: WAL size over time, checkpoint frequency,
     per-event p50/p95/p99/p99.9 latency.
   - Compare three PRAGMA configurations: default,
     `synchronous=NORMAL+wal_autocheckpoint=10000`, manual
     checkpoint via `wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)`.

2. **Configuration knobs in ft.toml**:
   ```toml
   [storage.wal]
   autocheckpoint_pages = 10000
   synchronous = "normal"   # or "full" / "off"
   journal_size_limit_mb = 64
   ```

3. **Telemetry counter** for checkpoint events:
   `counter!("storage.wal_checkpoint_ms")`. The diagnostic
   surface (per ft-40k1o's ft doctor goldens) shows the
   distribution.

4. **Backpressure integration**: when WAL size > threshold OR
   recent checkpoint took > N ms, raise to Yellow tier so
   ingest knows to throttle.

## Out of scope

- Migrating to a different storage engine (orthogonal — WAL is
  fine; the audit gap is the lack of measurement).
- The `frankensqlite` rollout-only backend (per README hedge).

## Acceptance criteria

- `benches/wal_checkpoint_cost.rs` exists and runs the three
  PRAGMA configurations.
- p99 latency under sustained 10K-events/sec load is documented
  in `docs/perf-ledger/wal-checkpoint.md`.
- `[storage.wal]` config block is wired to the production loader.
- Telemetry counter for checkpoint duration lands.
- Per the profiling skill's hand-off contract, this bead stops
  at the hotspot table; optimization (if needed) becomes a
  follow-on bead per `extreme-software-optimization` skill.

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs` (WAL mode setup)
- ft-9r82k (sibling: recorder ingest bench — covers
  serialize/flush, NOT WAL checkpoint specifically)
- README §Replay & Forensics
- Skill: profiling-software-performance — IO-AND-TRADEOFFS.md
  ("disk is the bottleneck" trigger; biolatency for fsync)

Filed by cc_1 codebase audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T10:27:13.143651Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:27:13.143651Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
